revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。 ==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *他8柱 ==由...'
revision.text:
'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *他8柱
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== この神社の例大祭（10月）は、播州三大祭のひとつである。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *他8柱
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== この神社の例大祭（10月）は、播州三大祭のひとつである。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] [[category:三木市|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *他8柱
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== この神社の例大祭（10月）は、播州三大祭のひとつである。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] [[category:三木市|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== この神社の例大祭（10月）は、播州三大祭のひとつである。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] [[category:三木市|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田・大村 *宮入休止中:城山町(担ぎ手不足のため)
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] [[category:三木市|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田(昼宮のみ)・大村(昼宮のみ) *宮入休止中:城山町(担ぎ手不足のため)
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
[[category:兵庫県の神社|おおみやはちまんくう]] [[category:三木市|おおみやはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田(昼宮のみ)・大村(昼宮のみ) *宮入休止中:城山町(担ぎ手不足のため)
尚、新町屋台は2007年に屋台が焼失したが、今後の対策は未定である。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]

==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
==外部リンク== *[http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]

'''{{nihongo|Omiya Hachiman Shrine|大宮八幡宮|Ohmiya-Hachimangu}}''' is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]] City, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
This shrine was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down. <ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Events==
*Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest Festival.
==References== <references/>
==Location==
This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu_Ao_Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]] .
{{Shinto-stub}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

'''{{nihongo|Omiya Hachiman Shrine|大宮八幡宮|Ohmiya-Hachimangu}}''' is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]] City, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
'''Omiya Hachiman Shrine''' in Hyogo is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down. <ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Events==
*Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest Festival.
==References== <references/>
==Location==
This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu_Ao_Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]] .
{{Shinto-stub}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため）
尚、新町屋台は2007年に屋台が焼失し、2007年の屋台巡行は断念となった。それ以後の対策は未定である。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:三木市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[en:Omiya Hachiman Shrine (Hyogo)]]

'''{{nihongo|Omiya Hachiman Shrine|大宮八幡宮|Ohmiya-Hachimangu}}''' is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]] City, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
'''Omiya Hachiman Shrine''' in Hyogo is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down. <ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Events==
*Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest Festival.
==References== {{reflist}}
==Location==
This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]] .
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Hyōgo Prefecture]]
{{Shinto-stub}}
[[ja:大宮八幡宮 (三木市)]]

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== [[画像:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|right|200px|大宮八幡宮 屋台[[宮入り]]]] 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・(新町)・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため）新町(屋台焼失のため) 尚、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。[[2009年]]を目処に屋台復活を予定している。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:三木市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

*宮入順:明石町・(新町)・全末広・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため）新町(屋台焼失のため) 尚、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成予定。屋台本体は[[2009年]]の9月にお披露目予定になっている。
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== [[画像:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|right|200px|大宮八幡宮 屋台[[宮入り]]]] 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・(新町)・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため）新町(屋台焼失のため) なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成した。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開する予定である。
==屋台== *明石町屋台 :平屋根3段 *新町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *全末広屋台 :平屋根3段 *下町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *栄町屋台 :平屋根3段 *高木屋台 :平屋根3段 *平田屋台 :平屋根3段 *大村屋台 :平屋根3段
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんぐう}} [[Category:三木市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}
[[en:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Hyōgo)]]

==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== [[画像:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|right|200px|大宮八幡宮 屋台[[宮入り]]]] 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・(新町)・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため）新町(屋台焼失のため) なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成した。屋台本体は同年8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開する予定である。
==屋台== *明石町屋台 :平屋根3段 *新町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *全末広屋台 :平屋根3段 *下町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *栄町屋台 :平屋根3段 *高木屋台 :平屋根3段 *平田屋台 :平屋根3段 *大村屋台 :平屋根3段
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮]

'''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]にある[[八幡宮]]。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]] *[[国水分神]] *[[天照大神]] *[[素盞鳴尊]] *[[天児屋根命]] *[[熊野久須昆命]] *[[賀茂別雷神]] *[[中筒之男命]] *[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。
==例祭== [[画像:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|right|200px|大宮八幡宮 屋台[[宮入り]]]] 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。

==例祭== [[画像:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|right|200px|大宮八幡宮 屋台[[宮入り]]]] 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==屋台== *明石町屋台 :平屋根3段 *新町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *全末広屋台 :平屋根3段 *下町屋台 :反り屋根3段 *栄町屋台 :平屋根3段 *高木屋台 :平屋根3段 *平田屋台 :平屋根3段 *大村屋台 :平屋根3段
==所在地== *兵庫県三木市本町2-19-1
== 外部リンク == * [http://www2.117.ne.jp/~oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮] {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}}

===例大祭=== 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。

==祭神== *主祭神 - [[応神天皇]] *配神 - [[水分神|国水分神]]、[[天照大神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[熊野久須昆命]]、[[賀茂別雷命|賀茂別雷神]]、[[住吉三神|中筒之男命]]、[[津御名方命]]
==由緒== この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。旧社格は[[郷社]]。
==主要建造物== *本殿 *拝殿 *境内摂社 **大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 *境内末社 **豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 *境外社 **三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>
==行事・祭事== [[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|鬼追式]] [[ファイル:Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine Mikoshi.JPG|thumb|例大祭]] *鬼追式（[[修正会]]の[[追儺式]]） - 1月第3日曜日 *とんど祭 - 1月15日 *例大祭 - 10月中旬（体育の日の前の土曜・日曜）
===例大祭=== 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
*宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） *宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
====屋台==== *明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 *新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 *全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 *下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 *栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 *高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 *平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 *大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == *[[八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.h-synergy.co.jp/oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮] * [http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/akimatsuri.html 三木秋まつり/三木市] {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんぐう}} [[Category:三木市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] {{shinto-stub}}

==主要建造物== *本殿 *拝殿 *境内摂社 **大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 *境内末社 **豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 *境外社 **三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>

=== 例大祭 === 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
* 宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） * 宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://www.h-synergy.co.jp/oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮] * [http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/akimatsuri.html 三木秋まつり/三木市]

{{神社 |名称=大宮八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] |所在地=兵庫県三木市本町二丁目19-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP}} |祭神=応神天皇（主祭神）ほか八神 |社格=郷社 |創建= |札所等= |例祭=[[10月]]中旬 |本殿= }} '''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]本町二丁目にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]] * 配神 - [[水分神|国水分神]]、[[天照大神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[熊野久須昆命]]、[[賀茂別雷命|賀茂別雷神]]、[[住吉三神|中筒之男命]]、[[津御名方命]]
== 由緒 == この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。旧社格は[[郷社]]。
== 主要建造物 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 境内摂社 ** 大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 * 境内末社 ** 豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 * 境外社 ** 三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>

* 宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） * 宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://www.h-synergy.co.jp/oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮] * [http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/akimatsuri.html 三木秋まつり/三木市]

== 行事・祭事 == [[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|鬼追式]] [[ファイル:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|例大祭]] * 鬼追式（[[修正会]]の[[追儺式]]） - 1月第3日曜日 * とんど祭 - 1月15日 * 例大祭 - 10月中旬（体育の日の前の土曜・日曜）
=== 例大祭 === 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
* 宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） * 宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://www.h-synergy.co.jp/oomiya/ 大宮八幡宮] * [http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/akimatsuri.html 三木秋まつり/三木市]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんぐう}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称=大宮八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] |所在地=兵庫県三木市本町二丁目19-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP}} |祭神=応神天皇（主祭神）ほか八神 |社格=郷社 |創建= |札所等= |例祭=[[10月]]中旬 |本殿= }} '''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]本町二丁目にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]] * 配神 - [[水分神|国水分神]]、[[天照大神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[熊野久須昆命]]、[[賀茂別雷命|賀茂別雷神]]、[[住吉三神|中筒之男命]]、[[津御名方命]]
== 由緒 == この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。旧社格は[[郷社]]。
== 主要建造物 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 境内摂社 ** 大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 * 境内末社 ** 豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 * 境外社 ** 三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>
== 行事・祭事 == [[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|鬼追式]] [[ファイル:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|例大祭]] * 鬼追式（[[修正会]]の[[追儺式]]） - 1月第3日曜日 * とんど祭 - 1月15日 * 例大祭 - 10月中旬（体育の日の前の土曜・日曜）

==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段

== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ 大宮八幡宮]（2017年9月20日閲覧） * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 三木市 [ 三木の秋まつり ]]（2017年9月20日閲覧） * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi 三木秋まつり 道の駅みき市]（2017年9月20日閲覧） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称=大宮八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] |所在地=兵庫県三木市本町二丁目19-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神=応神天皇（主祭神）ほか八神 |社格=郷社 |創建= |札所等= |例祭=[[10月]]中旬 |本殿= }} '''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]本町二丁目にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]] * 配神 - [[水分神|国水分神]]、[[天照大神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[熊野久須昆命]]、[[賀茂別雷命|賀茂別雷神]]、[[住吉三神|中筒之男命]]、[[津御名方命]]
== 由緒 == この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。旧社格は[[郷社]]。
== 主要建造物 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 境内摂社 ** 大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 * 境内末社 ** 豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 * 境外社 ** 三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>
== 行事・祭事 == [[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|鬼追式]] [[ファイル:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|例大祭]] * 鬼追式（[[修正会]]の[[追儺式]]） - 1月第3日曜日 * とんど祭 - 1月15日 * 例大祭 - 10月中旬（体育の日の前の土曜・日曜）
=== 例大祭 === 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
* 宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） * 宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ 大宮八幡宮]（2017年9月20日閲覧） * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 三木市 [ 三木の秋まつり ]]（2017年9月20日閲覧） * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi 三木秋まつり 道の駅みき市]（2017年9月20日閲覧） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称=大宮八幡宮 |画像=[[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]本町二丁目19-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]]（主祭神）ほか八神 |社格=郷社 |創建= |札所等= |例祭=[[10月]]中旬 |本殿= |地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=大宮八幡宮|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''大宮八幡宮'''（おおみやはちまんぐう）は、[[兵庫県]][[三木市]]本町二丁目にある[[神社]]。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]] * 配神 - [[水分神|国水分神]]、[[天照大神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]、[[熊野久須昆命]]、[[賀茂別雷命|賀茂別雷神]]、[[住吉三神|中筒之男命]]、[[津御名方命]]
== 由緒 == この神社の創建年代については不詳であるが、[[赤松氏]]の一族[[別所氏]]の崇拝を受けた。[[戦国時代_(日本)|戦国時代]]に焼失したが、[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]から[[朱印状]]を受けていた。旧社格は[[郷社]]。
== 主要建造物 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 境内摂社 ** 大宮天満宮、尾引稲荷社、伏見稲荷社 * 境内末社 ** 豊玉姫社、祓殿、播東稲荷社、弁財天社 * 境外社 ** 三本松稲荷社、金刀比羅社 <gallery> ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|大宮天満宮 ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|石段 </gallery>
== 行事・祭事 == [[ファイル:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|鬼追式]] [[ファイル:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|例大祭]] * 鬼追式（[[修正会]]の[[追儺式]]） - 1月第3日曜日 * とんど祭 - 1月15日 * 例大祭 - 10月中旬（体育の日の前の土曜・日曜）
=== 例大祭 === 毎年10月、[[体育の日]]の前の土曜日・日曜日に行われるこの神社の例大祭は、播州三大祭のひとつである。各地区の[[太鼓台|屋台]]8基が繰り出し、85段の急な石段を登って宮入りする。1トンを超える屋台が石段を登っていく様子は壮観である。なお、大村地区を除く7基の屋台は命綱をつけて宮入、宮出を行うが、大村地区だけは命綱をつけない。また、祭り屋台は、[[大工]]・[[刺繍]]・[[木彫]]・[[錺金具]]の伝統的な職人の技が一体となった非常に華麗なものである。
* 宮入順:明石町・新町・全末廣・下町・榮町・高木・平田（昼宮のみ）・大村（昼宮のみ） * 宮入休止中:城山町（担ぎ手不足のため） なお、新町屋台は[[2007年]]に屋台が焼失し、同年、翌年の屋台巡行は断念となった。新しい屋台蔵は[[2009年]]の2月に完成。屋台本体は8月に完成し、9月にお披露目された。また、同年より屋台巡行を再開。
==== 屋台 ==== * 明石町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 新町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 全末広屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 下町屋台 - 反り屋根3段 * 栄町屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 城山町屋台 - 平屋根3段（運行休止中） * 高木屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 平田屋台 - 平屋根3段 * 大村屋台 - 平屋根3段
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest Festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Overview==
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | Image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | Location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | Festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | Established = | Pilgrimage = | Annual Festival = Mid-[[October]] | Main Hall = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== Festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari-no-Kami|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|Main Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and Festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing Ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|Annual Festival]] * Oni-chasing Ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo Festival - January 15 * Annual Festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== Annual Festival === The shrine's annual festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers

== Main Structures == * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|Main Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and Festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing Ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest Festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|Annual Festival]] * Oni-chasing Ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo Festival - January 15 * Annual Festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== Annual Festival === The shrine's annual festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers

'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].

== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|Main Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>

=== Annual Festival === The shrine's annual festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.

{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | annual festival = Mid-[[October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari-no-Kami|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|annual festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * annual festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== annual festival === The shrine's annual festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

==Events== *Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest festival. [[ja:大宮八幡宮 (三木市)]]

==References== {{reflist}}
==location== This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]].

== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|annual festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * annual festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== annual festival === The shrine's annual festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.

'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari-no-Kami|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-[[October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari-no-Kami|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari-no-Kami|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers

'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine''' (おおみやはちまんぐう) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]].
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.

==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-[[October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-[[October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

==Events== *Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest festival. [[Category:kk]][[ja:大宮八幡宮 (三木市)]]
==See also== *[[Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Tokyo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==location== This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]].

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|42.1|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg|300px]] | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|47|42.2|N|134|59|8.4|E|region:JP-28|display=inline,title}} | festival Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-[[October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Jinja (Shinto)|shrine]] in [[Miki, Hyogo|Miki]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== Related Topics == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

==Events== *Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest festival. [[Category:kk]][[ja:大宮八幡宮 (三木市)]]
==See also== *[[Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Tokyo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==location== This shrine is near [[Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[Kobe Electric Railway]].

It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== See Also == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)
