It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== See Also == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

It is a [[Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyogo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[carpentry]], [[embroidery]], [[wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers
== See Also == * [[Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|qid=Q31238}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.795|N|134.986|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] | festival Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-[[:en:October|October]] | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>

Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)

== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]

Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)
{{Shinto temples}}
{{shinto-stub}}
==Events== *Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest festival.
==See also== *[[:en:Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)|Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==location== This shrine is near [[:en:Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[:en:Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]].

Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)
{{Shinto temples}}
{{shinto-stub}}
==Events== *Third Sunday in January: Oni-oi Shiki. *Second and third Saturdays and Sundays in October: Miki Autumn Harvest festival.
==See also== *[[:en:Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)|Ōmiya Hachimangū (Tokyo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==location== This shrine is near [[:en:Miki Station (Shintetsu)|Miki station]] on the [[:en:Shintetsu Ao Line|Ao Line]] of [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]].
== External links == *[http://miki-oomiya.net Official website] {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from wgoodbye $1 2]][[Category|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}}

Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] in [[:en:Miki, Hyōgo|Miki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the [[:en:Akamatsu clan|Akamatsu clan]]. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in [[:en:Banshū|Banshū]]. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of [[:en:Carpentry|Carpentry]], [[:en:Embroidery|Embroidery]], [[:en:Wood carving|Wood carving]], and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

Ōjin}} (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-{{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October}} | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} in {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|WD=Q31238|lt=Miki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|WD=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan}}. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in {{ill|Banshū|en|Banshū|WD=Q16255510|lt=Banshū}}. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=Carpentry}}, {{ill|Embroidery|en|Embroidery|WD=Q18281|lt=Embroidery}}, {{ill|Wood carving|en|Wood carving|WD=Q337907|lt=Wood carving}}, and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.795|N|134.986|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} | festival Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-{{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October}} | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} in {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|WD=Q31238|lt=Miki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|WD=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan}}. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in {{ill|Banshū|en|Banshū|WD=Q16255510|lt=Banshū}}. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=Carpentry}}, {{ill|Embroidery|en|Embroidery|WD=Q18281|lt=Embroidery}}, {{ill|Wood carving|en|Wood carving|WD=Q337907|lt=Wood carving}}, and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|WD=Q31238|lt=Miki|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|WD=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in {{ill|Banshū|en|Banshū|WD=Q16255510|lt=Banshū|1=Banshū}}. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=Carpentry|1=Carpentry}}, {{ill|Embroidery|en|Embroidery|WD=Q18281|lt=Embroidery|1=Embroidery}}, {{ill|Wood carving|en|Wood carving|WD=Q337907|lt=Wood carving|1=Wood carving}}, and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} | image = File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyogo16n4272.jpg | caption = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.795|N|134.986|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine | location = 2-19-1 Honmachi, [[Miki City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} | festival Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (main deity) and eight other deities | Shrine Rank = Gōsha (Village Shrine) | established = | Pilgrimage = | festival = Mid-{{ill|October|en|October|WD=Q124|lt=October|1=October}} | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|WD=Q31238|lt=Miki|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|WD=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in {{ill|Banshū|en|Banshū|WD=Q16255510|lt=Banshū|1=Banshū}}. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=Carpentry|1=Carpentry}}, {{ill|Embroidery|en|Embroidery|WD=Q18281|lt=Embroidery|1=Embroidery}}, {{ill|Wood carving|en|Wood carving|WD=Q337907|lt=Wood carving|1=Wood carving}}, and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine (Miki)}} * [http://miki-oomiya.net/ Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://www2.city.miki.lg.jp/miki.nsf/doc/3C6D0DAC490953EC49257FEA000B5A94 Miki City Autumn festival] (accessed September 20, 2017) * [http://mikiyama.co.jp/sightseeing/maturi Miki Autumn festival - Michi-no-Eki Miki City] (accessed September 20, 2017)

{{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=47|lat_sec=42.2|lon_deg=134|lon_min=59|lon_sec=8.4|label=Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Miki|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}} }} [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Miki Autumn Harvest festival]][[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo10n4272.jpg|thumb|Oni-oi Shiki]] {{nihongo|'''Ōmiya Hachiman Shrine'''|大宮八幡宮|Ōmiya-Hachimangū}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|WD=Q31238|lt=Miki|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] that was founded in 1111 and was rebuilt in 1585 after having burned down.<ref>[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/bunka/hachimangu.html Omiya Hachiman Shrine] on the Miki City website (Japanese)</ref>
== festival Deities == * Main deity - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Associated deities - [[Mikumari|Kuninomikumari-no-Kami]], [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Koyane]], [[Kumano Kusu-kun-no-Mikoto]], [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami]], [[Nakatsu-no-Ono-no-Mikoto|Nakatomi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto|Minakatatomi-no-Mikoto]]
== History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown, but it was worshipped by the [[Bessho clan]], a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|WD=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}. The shrine was burned down during the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], but in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it received a red seal from the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The former shrine Rank was [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine).
== Main Structures == * main hall * Worship Hall * Auxiliary Shrines within the grounds ** Ōmiya Tenman Shrine, Obiki Inari Shrine, Fushimi Inari Shrine * Subsidiary Shrines within the grounds ** Toyotama-hime Shrine, Harai-den, Bandō Inari Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine * External Shrines ** Sanbonmatsu Inari Shrine, Kotohira Shrine <gallery> File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo07n4272.jpg|main hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo02n4272.jpg|Worship Hall File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo03n4272.jpg|Ōmiya Tenman Shrine File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo01n4272.jpg|Stone Steps </gallery>
== Events and festivals == [[File:Omiya-hachimangu Miki Hyōgo11n3200.jpg|thumb|Oni-chasing ceremony]] [[File:121007 Miki Autumn Harvest festival Miki Hyōgo pref Japan02s5.jpg|thumb|festival]] * Oni-chasing ceremony (Setsubun Ritual) - 3rd Sunday in January * Tondo festival - January 15 * festival - Mid-October (Saturday and Sunday before Sports Day)
=== festival === The shrine's festival held every October on the Saturday and Sunday before [[:en:Sports Day|Sports Day]] is one of the three major festivals in {{ill|Banshū|en|Banshū|WD=Q16255510|lt=Banshū|1=Banshū}}. Eight floats from different districts are paraded and ascend the 85 steep stone steps to the shrine. The sight of the over one-ton floats being carried up the steps is impressive. Except for the Ōmura district, all seven other floats are tied with safety ropes during the ascent and descent. The festival floats are decorated with traditional crafts of {{ill|Carpentry|en|Carpentry|WD=Q203605|lt=Carpentry|1=Carpentry}}, {{ill|Embroidery|en|Embroidery|WD=Q18281|lt=Embroidery|1=Embroidery}}, {{ill|Wood carving|en|Wood carving|WD=Q337907|lt=Wood carving|1=Wood carving}}, and [[metal fittings]].
* Parade Order: Akashi Town, Shinmachi, Zen Suehiro, Shitamachi, Sakaemachi, Takagi, Hirata (only during the day), Ōmura (only during the day) * Parade Suspended: Shiroyama Town (due to a shortage of carriers)
Note: The Shinmachi float was destroyed by fire in 2007, and the parade was canceled that year and the following year. A new float storage was completed in February 2009, and the new float was unveiled in September of the same year. The parade resumed that year.
==== Floats ==== * Akashi Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shinmachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Zen Suehiro Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shitamachi Float - Curved roof, three tiers * Sakaemachi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Shiroyama Town Float - Flat roof, three tiers (parade suspended) * Takagi Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Hirata Float - Flat roof, three tiers * Ōmura Float - Flat roof, three tiers == See Also == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]
